content.query:
-- This is auto-generated code
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'https://datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/nyc-taxi-data/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0'
    ) AS [result]

-- how to use abfss path for above query
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',  --PARSER_VERSION tells Synapse which CSV parsing engine to use when reading CSV files via OPENROWSET
        header_row = true,
        FIELDTERMINATOR = ',',  --comma is a default terminator
        ROWTERMINATOR = '\n'
    ) AS [result];

-- examine the data types for the columns 
-- examine the data types for the columns
EXEC sp_describe_first_result_set N'
SELECT
    TOP 100 *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK ''abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv'',
        FORMAT = ''CSV'',
        PARSER_VERSION = ''2.0'',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]
'

-- To check max length of each of the column
SELECT
    MAX(LEN(LocationID)) as len_locationid,
    MAX(LEN(Borough)) as len_borough,
    MAX(LEN(Zone)) as len_zone,
    MAX(len(service_zone)) as len_service_zone
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]

-- Use with clause to provide explicit datasets
SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        LocationID SMALLINT,
        Borough VARCHAR(15),
        Zone VARCHAR(50),
        service_zone VARCHAR(15)
    ) AS [result]

EXEC sp_describe_first_result_set N'
SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK ''abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv'',
        FORMAT = ''CSV'',
        PARSER_VERSION = ''2.0'',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        LocationID SMALLINT,
        Borough VARCHAR(15),
        Zone VARCHAR(50),
        service_zone VARCHAR(15)
    ) AS [result]
'

-- Check default collation
SELECT name, collation_name from sys.databases;

-- Specify UTF-8 Collation for varchar columns
SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        LocationID SMALLINT,
        Borough VARCHAR(15) COLLATE Latin1_General_100_CI_AI_SC_UTF8,
        Zone VARCHAR(50) COLLATE Latin1_General_100_CI_AI_SC_UTF8,
        service_zone VARCHAR(15) COLLATE Latin1_General_100_CI_AI_SC_UTF8
    ) AS [result]

-- We can specify/apply UTF-8 Collation at Database level too (You can't alter Master Database)
CREATE DATABASE nyc_taxi_discovery;

USE nyc_taxi_discovery;

ALTER DATABASE nyc_taxi_discovery COLLATE Latin1_General_100_CI_AI_SC_UTF8;

SELECT name, collation_name from sys.databases;

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        LocationID SMALLINT,
        Borough VARCHAR(15),
        Zone VARCHAR(50),
        service_zone VARCHAR(15)
    ) AS [result]

---------------------------------- Query Subset of Columns ------------------------------

-------------------------------- Hor to Query Subset of Columns -------------------------------------
use nyc_taxi_discovery;

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        Borough VARCHAR(15),
        Zone VARCHAR(50)
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone_without_header.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        FIELDTERMINATOR = ',',
        ROWTERMINATOR = '\n'
    )
    WITH(
        Borough VARCHAR(15) 2,             --- use ordinal positions to map column names correctly with the columns with no headers
        Zone VARCHAR(50) 3
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        Location_id SMALLINT,  --- case sensitivity of the column name changed - we get null values
        borough VARCHAR(15),   --- case sensitivity of the column name changed - we get null values
        Zone VARCHAR(50),
        service_zone VARCHAR(15)
    ) AS [result];

---- If we assign ordinal position then we will get correct values even with different case-sensitivity column names
SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        Location_id SMALLINT 1,  
        borough VARCHAR(15) 2,   
        zone VARCHAR(50) 3,
        service_zone VARCHAR(15) 4
    ) AS [result];

----------------------------------- HOW TO DEBUG ISSUES --------------------------------------

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    )
    WITH(
        Location_id SMALLINT 1,  
        borough VARCHAR(15) 2,   
        zone VARCHAR(5) 3,                --- MOST OF THE TIME, WE GIVE INCORRECT DATA TYPES. HERE INSTEAD OF 50 WE HAVE GIVEN VARCHAR(5). ERROR MESSAGE IS VERY GENERIC 
        service_zone VARCHAR(15) 4
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '1.0',  --- WE SWITCHED TO VERSION 1.0 -- ERROR IS MORE SPECIFIC
        FIRSTROW = 2
    )
    WITH(
        LocationID SMALLINT,
        Borough VARCHAR(15),
        Zone VARCHAR(5),              
        service_zone VARCHAR(15) 4
    ) AS [result];

---------------------------- HOW TO USE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE --------------------------------------
CREATE EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE nyc_taxi_data
WITH (
    LOCATION = 'abfss://nyc-taxi-data@datatocrunchproject8adls.dfs.core.windows.net/'
);

SELECT
    *
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/taxi_zone.csv',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        FIRSTROW = 2
    )
    WITH(
        Location_id SMALLINT 1,  
        borough VARCHAR(15) 2,   
        zone VARCHAR(50) 3,               
        service_zone VARCHAR(15) 4
    ) AS [result];

SELECT name, location from sys.external_data_sources;

use nyc_taxi_discovery;

select
    * 
from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'raw/calendar.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    HEADER_ROW = true
) as cal;

exec sp_describe_first_result_set N'
select
    * 
from OPENROWSET(
    BULK ''raw/calendar.csv'',
    DATA_SOURCE = ''nyc_taxi_data'',
    FORMAT = ''csv'',
    PARSER_VERSION = ''2.0'',
    HEADER_ROW = true
) as cal
'

select
    * 
from OPENROWSET(
    BULK 'raw/calendar.csv',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    HEADER_ROW = true
) with (
    date_key int,
    date date,
    year smallint,
    month tinyint,
    day tinyint,
    day_name varchar(10),
    day_of_year smallint,
    week_of_month tinyint,
    week_of_year tinyint,
    month_name varchar(10),
    year_month int,
    year_week int
)as cal;

use nyc_taxi_discovery;

-------------------------- single line JSON ----------------------------
select 
    *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

select 
    JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as payment_type,
    JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc') as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

------------------------ declare data type for each column using CAST ------------------------------
select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

--- json_value has some limitations - 1. it is difficult to define the data types for the column  
--- 2. if you have an array within json document, you can go and pick a perticular specific occurrence of an array but you cant really explode the entire array
--- 3. also not the efficient function
--- Hence synapse provides an alternative called open_json - table valued function which takes an adjacent document & turns its objects & properties into rows & columns
--- It is more efficient than json_value when you are dealing with huge amount of data
--- You can define data types instead of casting , you can list the columns & rename it if required
select 
     *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
     payment_type,
     payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
     payment_type,
     description
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f'
    --ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    description VARCHAR(20) '$.payment_type_desc'
);

--------------------------- Explore JSON Array -----------------------
select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[0].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

select 
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type') as SMALLINT) as payment_type,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[0].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc_0,
    CAST(JSON_VALUE(jsonDoc,'$.payment_type_desc[1].value') as VARCHAR(15)) as payment_type_desc_1
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type;

----------- Use open_json to explode array 

select 
    *
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH
(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc NVARCHAR(MAX) as json
)
CROSS APPLY openjson(payment_type_desc)
WITH(
    sub_type SMALLINT,
    value VARCHAR(20)
);

select 
    payment_type,
    sub_type,
    value
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/payment_type_array.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x1f',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0a'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as payment_type
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH
(
    payment_type SMALLINT,
    payment_type_desc NVARCHAR(MAX) as json
)
CROSS APPLY openjson(payment_type_desc)
WITH(
    sub_type SMALLINT,
    value VARCHAR(20)
);


---------------------------------------- Query Standard JSON --------------------------
select 
     rate_code_id,
     rate_code
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/rate_code.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as rate_code
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    rate_code_id TINYINT,
    rate_code VARCHAR(20)
);

------------ Query Classic JSON - these are same as standard json when we use serverless sql pool -----------------
select 
     rate_code_id,
     rate_code
from OPENROWSET (
    BULK 'raw/rate_code_multi_line.json',
    DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
    FORMAT = 'csv',
    --PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
    FIELDTERMINATOR = '0x0b',
    FIELDQUOTE = '0x0b',
    ROWTERMINATOR = '0x0b'
)
with (
    jsonDoc VARCHAR(8000)
) as rate_code
CROSS APPLY openjson(jsonDoc)
WITH(
    rate_code_id TINYINT,
    rate_code VARCHAR(20)
);

USE nyc_taxi_discovery;

-- This is auto-generated code
SELECT
    result.filepath() as file_path,
    result.*    
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=2020/month=01/green_tripdata_2020-01.csv',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    result.filepath() as file_path,
    result.*    
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=2020/**',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    result.filepath() as file_path,
    result.*    
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK ('raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=2020/month=01/*','raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=2020/month=03/*'),
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    result.filepath() as file_path,
    result.filename() as file_name,
    count(*) OVER() AS total_dataset_records,
    result.*    
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=*/month=*/*',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result];

SELECT
    result.filename() as file_name,
    count(*) as record_count   
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=*/month=*/*',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]
GROUP BY result.filename()
order by result.filename();

SELECT
    result.filename() as file_name,
    count(*) as record_count   
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=*/month=*/*',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]
WHERE result.filename() IN ('green_tripdata_2021-03.csv','green_tripdata_2021-06.csv')
GROUP BY result.filename()
order by result.filename();

SELECT
    result.filename() as file_name,
    result.filepath(1) as year,
    result.filepath(2) as month,
    count(*) as record_count   
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=*/month=*/*',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]
WHERE result.filename() IN ('green_tripdata_2021-03.csv','green_tripdata_2021-06.csv')
GROUP BY result.filename(), result.filepath(1),result.filepath(2)
order by result.filename(), result.filepath(1),result.filepath(2);

SELECT
    result.filepath(1) as year,
    result.filepath(2) as month,
    count(*) as record_count   
FROM
    OPENROWSET(
        BULK 'raw/trip_data_green_csv/year=*/month=*/*',
        DATA_SOURCE = 'nyc_taxi_data',
        FORMAT = 'CSV',
        PARSER_VERSION = '2.0',
        HEADER_ROW = TRUE
    ) AS [result]
GROUP BY  result.filepath(1),result.filepath(2)
order by result.filepath(1),result.filepath(2);
